Her Majesty’s government also thought that the United States must be aware that the law of nations and the circumstances of the war gave an immense advantage to the federalists against the confederates in obtaining warlike supplies.

In confirmation of this remark, it may be reckoned that besides very many batteries of artillery, five hundred thousand rifles have been manufactured in this country and conveyed to the shores of the northern States, to be used by the federal troops in the war.
